articles.title: Odd Lots: Making the Hay Market More Transparent (Podcast)
articles.summary: The hay market is not a transparent market: It is very fractured by types of hay, whether it is alfalfa or clover hay. There are a few opaque, illiquid markets like this — scrap metal for instance — that require some hands-on investigating to figure out. articles.title: Stocks surge after Trump signals end to planned Iran strikes
articles.summary: Stocks surge after Trump signals end to planned Iran strikes Global stock markets rallied after US President Donald Trump said he had cancelled a third day of planned strikes against Iran and indicated that an agreement with Tehran was close. Investors welcomed signs of de-escalation, helping lift shares across Wall Street and Asia. The S&P 500 posted its largest one-day gain since April, while the Nasdaq Composite rose 2.5 per cent and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 1.9 per cent. articles.summary: Central banks are staring down a pivotal moment for global monetary policy as they grapple with a growing list of risks. Incoming Fed Chair, Kevin Warsh will need to find a way to navigate the central bank’s policy path amidst a combination of higher energy prices and a surprisingly strong US jobs report. Meanwhile in Europe, the ECB and BOE face the prospect of secondary effects due to the war in Iran and a sluggish growth outlook. The Opening Trade spoke to key voices around the challenges facing central banks and the judgment behind the decisions. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.summary: Israeli attacks hit areas in Lebanon's Marjayoun and Nabatieh Israeli air strikes targeted the agricultural area of Arid Dbeibine near the southern Lebanese town of Dbeibine in the Marjayoun district, according to Lebanon’s National News Agency (NNA). The reported attack followed an overnight Israeli strike on the nearby town of Khiam. Separately, NNA and Al Jazeera reported an Israeli drone strike on the town of Jebchit in Lebanon’s Nabatieh district. There was no immediate information on casualties or the extent of the damage. articles.title: Cyber group claims California water system hack in retaliation for Iran strikes
articles.summary: Cyber group claims California water system hack in retaliation for Iran strikes A hacker-activist group, Handala, has claimed responsibility for a cyber intrusion targeting water facilities in California, saying the action was carried out in retaliation for alleged US strikes on water infrastructure in southern Iran, Press TV reported.  The group said it had obtained data from the systems and described the breach as a warning to Washington. In a statement, Handala said it had the capability to disrupt water supplies but "stopped short of actually cutting off water to American cities"

articles.title: NYT: US plans major reduction of military assets assigned to Nato
articles.summary: NYT: US plans major reduction of military assets assigned to Nato The United States plans to significantly reduce the number of aircraft and naval assets it makes available to Nato operations in Europe, according to a report by The New York Times, which cited two senior European officials. The newspaper said allies were informed of the decision in a written document circulated earlier this month, describing it as part of a broader shift in Washington’s security posture towards Europe. articles.title: Iranian migrants included in planned deportations to Central African Republic
articles.summary: Iranian migrants included in planned deportations to Central African Republic The administration of US President Donald Trump is planning to deport a group of migrants, including Iranian nationals, to the Central African Republic. The move comes as part of an arrangement that would allow the country to receive deportees from third countries, according to a Reuters report citing sources and lawyers familiar with the matter. Reuters reported that the group includes two Iranian women who had previously been granted legal protection in the United States. Details about the timing of the deportation

articles.title: Two UN peacekeepers wounded in attack on convoy in southern Lebanon
articles.summary: Two UN peacekeepers wounded in attack on convoy in southern Lebanon Two Unifil peacekeepers from Malaysia were wounded in an attack on a logistics convoy near the village of Haris in southern Lebanon, according to UN spokesperson Stephane Dujarric. Dujarric said the peacekeepers were in stable condition and that Unifil would investigate the incident. He did not identify who was responsible for the attack. articles.title: Report says Washington and Tehran preparing negotiations during ceasefire
articles.summary: Report says Washington and Tehran preparing negotiations during ceasefire The United States and Iran are expected to hold nuclear negotiations during a ceasefire period as part of a broader memorandum of understanding that would address Tehran’s stockpile of enriched uranium, according to a report by Axios citing an unnamed source. The report said the proposed arrangement includes a 60-day extension of the ceasefire, including in Lebanon, in exchange for sanctions relief if Iran complies with its commitments.
